topics: 🔄 Switched vessels at the artisan vs. mourner's house
🐴 Publicans, robbers, and yi'ush (despair) transferring ownership
🐝 Swarm of bees: pursuit into another's field, cutting branches
📚 Recognizing stolen vessels/books — when the buyer swears
💧 Saving honey vs. wine, water vs. honey in the desert

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Yi'ush, Rescue, and Switched Property
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: Daf 43 covers the second half of Chapter 10's mishnayot on rescue and recovery. The sugya develops yi'ush (the owner's despair) as the legal mechanism that transfers ownership when publicans seize a donkey, robbers take a garment, or a swarm of bees flies away. It then turns to recognizing stolen vessels in another's hand, and the moral economy of pouring out one's own wine to save another's honey — and water vs. honey in the desert.
overview.hebrewSource: נָטְלוּ מוֹכְסִין אֶת חֲמוֹרוֹ וְנָתְנוּ לוֹ חֲמוֹר אַחֵר... הֲרֵי אֵילּוּ שֶׁלּוֹ מִפְּנֵי שֶׁהַבְּעָלִין מִתְיָיאֲשִׁין מֵהֶן

slides.title: 🔄 Where Were the Vessels Switched?
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah distinguishes between an artisan's shop and a public gathering.

boxes.title: 🛠️ At the Artisan
items: Then go out and search for his own

boxes.title: 🕯️ Mourner's House / Wedding
items: אַל יִשְׁתַּמֵּשׁ בָּהֶן — may NOT use
Just go out and search for his own
No authorization implied in such gatherings

boxes.title: 🏪 Bar Qora's Shop
items: R' Hiyya's example via R' Abba bar Hanan
Every taking and depositing is by permission

callout.label: Logic:
callout.text: Use is permitted only where the holder of the items has implicit authority to grant it.

slides.title: 🐴 Yi'ush — The Engine of Ownership Transfer
slides.subtitle: The Mishnah at 10:2 lays out the principle and then applies it.

nodes.content: Item taken by force or lost
Yi'ush + change of hand = the finder/taker becomes owner.

callout.label: Beraita extension:
callout.text: Saved from an army, a fire, a sea-whirlpool, or a river-whirlpool — also his.

slides.title: 🐝 The Swarm of Bees and Joshua's Stipulation
slides.subtitle: May the beekeeper enter another's field to retrieve his swarm?

lines.speaker: M
lines.text: Mishnah: he may enter the field and take his swarm. If he caused damage, he pays — אֲבָל לֹא יָקוֹץ אֶת הַסּוֹכָה, but he may not cut a branch on condition to pay.

lines.speaker: RY
lines.text: R' Yishmael b. R' Yochanan b. Beroka: he may even cut and pay.

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: It is a stipulation of the court — שֶׁעַל מְנָת כֵּן הִנְחִיל יְהוֹשֻׁעַ לְיִשְׂרָאֵל אֶת הָאָרֶץ — Joshua distributed the Land on this very condition.

lines.speaker: RH
lines.text: R' Hinena b. Pappos in R' Yochanan's name: only when taken in flight, and only immediately. If it left and returned, it was said only out of fear or seduction.

callout.label: Big idea:
callout.text: Some property rules are not biblical — they are a stipulation built into the original distribution of the Land.

slides.title: 📚 Recognizing One's Own Vessels in Another's Hand
slides.subtitle: When can you reclaim books or vessels you recognize?
rows: Why do we make him swear?
שֶׁלֹּא יְהוּ בַעֲלֵי בָתִּים נִטְפָּלִין לַגַּנָּבִים — so householders won't fence stolen goods
Only if it is known the books were stolen
Must bring proof he stayed there that very night
If he says 'I bought from So-and-so' — believed
בַּר נַשׁ דָּעַקִּין — known troublemaker; suspect

callout.label: Tension:
callout.text: Protect the original owner — but don't turn every honest buyer into a presumed thief.

slides.title: 💧 Saving the Other's Cargo — Wages or Sale?
slides.subtitle: Two travelers on the road. The honey leaks. The water leaks in the desert.

steps.content: 1️⃣ Default: if he silently pours out his wine to save the honey, אֵין לוֹ אֶלָּא שְׂכָרוֹ — he gets only wages.
2️⃣ But if he says אַצִּיל אֶת שֶׁלָּךְ וְאַתָּה נוֹתֵן לִי דְמֵי שֶׁלִּי — he gets the full value.
3️⃣ In the desert, water beats honey: תְּנַיי בֵּית דִּין הוּא שֶׁיִּשְׁפּוֹךְ זֶה דִבְשׁוֹ — court stipulation forces the honey-holder to pour out for the water.
4️⃣ A river sweeps two donkeys; if one abandons his to save the more valuable other's, only wages — unless he stipulates first.
5️⃣ Yi'ush dispute: If he was crying 'I did not despair' — Reish Lakish says no yi'ush; R' Yochanan says חֲזָקָה מְיָיאֵשׁ הוּא — presumption of despair stands.

callout.label: Principle:
callout.text: Words matter. Saving without a stipulation is volunteer work; saving with a stipulation is a contract.

summary.principle: Daf 43 unifies switched-property, theft-by-force, lost swarms, and rescue cases under one engine: yi'ush plus a clear declaration determines who owns what — and Joshua's stipulation, the court's stipulation, and the desert's hierarchy override pure property right when life is at stake.

flowSteps.content:
Owner separated from item
switched, taken, lost, or in danger
Examine: did the owner give up hope?

🎓 Result:
Ownership shifts only when the law recognizes despair plus a change of hand — and rescuers must speak before they spill.

quiz.q: Why may one use mistakenly switched vessels at the artisan but not at a wedding?
o: The artisan implicitly authorizes use of the switched item; a wedding/mourner's house does not
Weddings are exempt from monetary rules
Artisans are presumed to be thieves
quiz.e: R' Hiyya's example of Bar Qora: every taking and depositing in such a shop is בִּרְשׁוּת.

quiz.q: What legal mechanism transfers ownership when publicans seize a donkey?
quiz.e: מִפְּנֵי שֶׁהַבְּעָלִין מִתְיָיאֲשִׁין מֵהֶן — the owners give up hope.

quiz.q: According to R' Yishmael b. R' Yochanan b. Beroka, on what basis may one cut a branch to retrieve a swarm?
o: A court stipulation rooted in Joshua's distribution of the Land
quiz.e: שֶׁעַל מְנָת כֵּן הִנְחִיל יְהוֹשֻׁעַ לְיִשְׂרָאֵל אֶת הָאָרֶץ.

quiz.q: Why does the Mishnah require the holder of recognized stolen books to swear at all (per R' Abba bar Mamal)?
o: So that householders will not become fences for thieves
quiz.e: שֶׁלֹּא יְהוּ בַעֲלֵי בָתִּים נִטְפָּלִין לַגַּנָּבִים.

quiz.q: If a man's donkey is being swept away and he cries 'I did not despair!', what does R' Yochanan rule?
o: חֲזָקָה מְיָיאֵשׁ הוּא — presumption stands that he despaired
Reish Lakish's view that crying proves no yi'ush is followed
quiz.e: R' Yochanan holds the presumption of yi'ush stands even against the cry; Reish Lakish disagrees.
